# CSS variables for theming in Reports.css (2026-03-10 11:12Z)

_**User**_

Read and update `d:\web development\test\src\pages\Reports.css` to use CSS variables for theming. Replace these hardcoded colors:

Color → Variable mapping:
- `#0f172a` → `var(--bg-page)`
- `#1e293b` → `var(--bg-surface)` or `var(--bg-input)` for input backgrounds
- `#334155` → `var(--border-default)` for borders, `var(--bg-elevated)` for backgrounds
- `#475569` → `var(--border-subtle)`
- `#64748b` → `var(--text-placeholder)`
- `#94a3b8` → `var(--text-muted)`
- `#cbd5e1` → `var(--text-label)`
- `#e2e8f0` → `var(--text-secondary)`
- `#f1f5f9` → `var(--text-primary)`

DO NOT change accent/status colors (blues, greens, reds, ambers, purples). Those stay the same.
DO NOT change rgba() values with colored channels.
Only change rgba(0,0,0,...) overlays where appropriate.

Use the StrReplace tool for each change.

---

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Updating color variables**

I'm replacing hardcoded colors with CSS variables for consistent theming. I'm adjusting the color mapping for #1e293b from var(--bg-surface) to ensure proper contrast in light theme. I'm using var(--bg-surface) for the color mapping, which will stay dark in dark mode but appear as white in light mode. I'm reconsidering the text color for rank badges; using `var(--bg-surface)` for dark/light themes might make white text invisible on gold background in light mode. I'll keep the gradient colors as they use neutral grays, not accent colors, and update the text color mapping to `var(--bg-surface)` for rank badges. I'm changing the rank badge colors to use the new text color variables for better theme consistency. 

I'm replacing hardcoded colors with new CSS variables for background and text colors. </details></think>
